business.headline: Sensex opens 600 points lower, Nifty below 23,700 as crude oil crosses $100Sensex opens 600 points lower, Nifty below 23,700 as crude oil crosses $100

AI bill catches up with Wall Street: Tesla crashes 14%, Alphabet falls 7%Tesla and Alphabet shares fell sharply after earnings highlighted the rising cost of AI investment. The reaction signalled that Wall Street is demanding clearer returns, cash flow and profit visibility.

Adani denies reports of airline plans, says not evaluating entry into aviationAdani Enterprises has denied reports that it is evaluating an entry into the airline business. The clarification follows speculation over rule changes and the group's wider aviation ambitions.

Why India's biggest airlines are worried about Adani's aviation ambitionsAdani's possible airline entry has prompted opposition from IndiGo and Air India. Both carriers say airport owners should remain neutral because they control vital infrastructure.

Will US scrap 10% tariff on India? Key deadline ends todayIf no new announcement is made, imports from India and several other US trading partners will revert to the normal Most Favoured Nation (MFN) tariff structure that was in place before April 2, 2025.
Crude oil crosses $100 for first time in two months as US-Iran conflict intensifiesAt the time of writing, Brent Crude, the global oil benchmark, was trading at $100.14 a barrel, up 6.45%.

Infosys to roll out salary hikes from October, hire 20,000 freshersThe IT major said the salary hike cycle will be implemented in three parts. While the company did not share details of the percentage increase, it confirmed that the rollout will begin in October and continue into January for different employee groups.

Infosys posts 12% jump in Q1 profit, trims upper end of FY27 growth outlookThe IT major posted a consolidated net profit of Rs 7,769 crore for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, compared with Rs 6,921 crore in the same period last year. Revenue from operations rose 14% to Rs 48,211 crore, up from Rs 42,279 crore a year ago.

Infosys appoints Ashiss Kumar Dash as CEO-designateAccording to the company, Dash will begin his role as CEO-designate with immediate effect. The Board has also approved its intention to appoint him as the Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director from April 1, 2027.

Crude oil nears $100: Why rising price is bad news for IndiaAt the time of writing, Brent crude was trading at $98.28 per barrel, up 4.48%, reflecting growing concerns over the geopolitical situation in the Middle East.

Retaining users is really hard: Nithin Kamath on broking business' biggest challengeKamath said one of the biggest hurdles for stockbrokers is user retention. While many people open trading accounts, not all continue investing or trading for years.

Sensex, Nifty extend losing streak to 4th day as Brent crude nears $100The BSE Sensex fell 363.66 points, or 0.47%, to close at 76,391.39, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 126.65 points, or 0.53%, to settle at 23,869.60. This marked Dalal Street's longest losing streak in seven weeks.

How FIFA turned the football World Cup into a $15 billion money machineFIFA used the expanded World Cup, higher ticket prices and new sales channels to drive record revenue. The tournament showed how nearly every part of football's biggest event is now being monetised.

JPMorgan to add 1,000 tech jobs across India GCCs despite AI-driven workforce changesThe new hiring will be aimed at technology specialists with expertise in cloud architecture, cybersecurity and AI data pipelines.

Don't click 'submit' yet! These 3 last-minute ITR checks are a mustFiling your ITR before the deadline is important, but filing it correctly is even more important. These three last-minute checks can help you avoid costly errors.

Physics Wallah founder Alakh Pandey joins Jantar Mantar protestAlakh Pandey joined the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar and urged the government to speak with students. His intervention came after criticism over his silence and widened the row beyond NEET to education and recruitment.

Uber layoffs: Company cuts 10% of customer service jobs as AI adoption growsUber has cut 10% of jobs in its customer service division and asked some remote staff to relocate to office hubs. The move marks the company's clearest link yet between AI adoption, organisational simplification and workforce changes.

Adani seeks rule change to launch airline, challenge IndiGo-Air India duopolyAdani Group has asked the government to relax a rule that limits airport operators' stakes in scheduled airlines. The proposal could open the door to a new carrier even as officials weigh safeguards and existing airlines raise conflict concerns.

Sensex, Nifty extend losses as Middle East tensions push oil past $95The BSE Sensex fell 314.62 points, or 0.41%, to 76,440.43, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 82.50 points, or 0.34%, to 23,913.75 around 9:30 am.

Google posts blockbuster quarter but Wall Street is worried about the billAlphabet reported a strong June quarter led by record Google Cloud growth and higher advertising revenue. Even so, investors focused on its bigger AI spending plan and the delayed Gemini rollout.

entertainment.headline: It's good to be back: Johnny Depp returns as Scrooge in Ebenezer trailer Paramount Pictures has released the first trailer for Ebenezer, with Johnny Depp playing Ebenezer Scrooge. The film marks his first major Hollywood studio release since the 2022 Amber Heard defamation trial.

Not easy to play Ram: Yash praises Ranbir, shares Ramayana challenges at Comic-Con Actors Ranbir Kapoor and Yash joined director Nitesh Tiwari and producer Namit Malhotra at San Diego Comic-Con 2026 to present Ramayana. Yash's praise for Ranbir's portrayal of Lord Ram and his remarks on the vision behind the project highlighted the film's ambitious global rollout.

Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya review: A warm and timely look at India's education systemRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026 Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya follows underpaid teachers navigating life inside a crumbling government school. The series uses humour and small heartbreaks to examine India's fractured education system.

The Odyssey box office Day 7: Nolan epic overtakes Oppenheimer globally Filmmaker Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is on the verge of crossing the Rs 100 crore mark in India within its first week of release. The Matt Damon-led epic has not only stayed ahead of Nolan's previous blockbuster Oppenheimer at the same stage but is also making a strong run at the North American box office.

Ranbir-Yash's Ramayana trailer launch postponed, makers plan global rollout The makers of Ramayana have postponed the trailer launch after partnering with Sony Pictures Entertainment for a global release. Namit Malhotra said the delay is part of a worldwide strategy and called it a landmark moment for Indian cinema.

Jana Nayagan box office Day 1: Vijay's farewell roar earns Rs 41 crore Thalapathy Vijay's Jana Nayagan opened strongly in India and overseas on day one. The start comes with added focus as it is his final theatrical release before he shifts to full-time politics.

Shahid Kapoor backs student protests, questions youth's fading faith in education Actor Shahid Kapoor has backed the student protests over the NEET-UG paper leak with an Instagram post questioning the education system. His remarks added to wider calls for accountability, reforms and restoring young people's faith in education.

Sohail Khan uses keyboard for first time on Alliance; internet calls him privileged Actor Sohail Khan's candid confession of never having used a keyboard before joining The Alliance has taken social media by surprise. Many reacted to the clip on Instagram, labelling him 'privileged'.

Salman, Alia Bhatt to Ananya Panday: Bollywood rallies behind protesting students The ongoing student protests over the NEET-UG paper leak have garnered widespread support from the Indian film industry, with celebrities urging for accountability and peaceful demonstrations. Salman Khan, Alia Bhatt, Ananya Panday, and Varun Dhawan, among others have joined the list.

He abused me: TV actor Saanvie Tallwar alleges on-set assault by Karan Kundrra TV actor Saanvie Tallwar has accused former co-star Karan Kundrra of harassment and assault during the shooting of Yeh Kahan Aa Gaye Hum. Kundrra has not responded, and Tallwar says the incident left the set atmosphere toxic.

Don't extend protest, will send food from home: Salman Khan to 'bro' Wangchuk Salman Khan has urged students protesting the NEET-UG paper leak to return home, assuring their safety and expressing confidence in strict action against the culprits. He also appealed to social activist Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike, offering to send him home-cooked food.

Did Rajkummar Rao really comment being 'under pressure' amid students' protest? After backing students protesting the alleged NEET-UG paper leak, actor Rajkummar Rao's alleged, now-deleted reply to a comment about the 'Modi Hai Toh Mumkin Hai' song sparked fresh debate. His mention of "pressure" prompted widespread speculation, though he did not clarify what he meant.

We watched Jana Nayagan: Every political reference we saw, and didn't in Vijay film Vijay's Jana Nayagan reached theatres after months of censor changes that removed or muted TVK references. The final cut keeps its political undertones while adding markers of Vijay's rise from actor to Chief Minister.

We owe it to them: Kareena says students deserve better, backs nationwide protests Kareena Kapoor has backed students protesting across India over the NEET paper leak and education reforms. Her statement adds a prominent film voice to demands for accountability and a credible merit-based system.

Rakhi Sawant has tears, questions and message for PM over students' protest. Video Rakhi Sawant posted a tearful video backing students after the Chalo Sansad clashes in Delhi. Her intervention has intensified online focus on the NEET 2026 paper leak protest and police response.

The Apartment Job review: Ji Sung leads a crime comedy that embraces the absurdRelease Date:Sat Jul 11, 2026 Netflix's The Apartment Job follows a former gang boss targeting an apartment reserve fund through residents' politics. The series turns petty housing disputes into a sharp backdrop for crime, greed and absurd comedy.

Internet questions timing of Rang De Basanti star Madhavan's 'safe' post on protest R Madhavan backed students protesting over the NEET paper leak and urged action against those responsible. His delayed statement drew sharp online criticism, with many users calling it too late and too safe.

Kota Factory to Aspirants: OTT saw India's education crisis coming before Bollywood As students protest examination irregularities at Jantar Mantar, OTT dramas on education feel newly urgent. Their stories echo the pressure, uncertainty and exhaustion many young aspirants say they live with.

Sonu Nigam, visibly irritated, refuses all questions on student protest. Video Sonu Nigam refused to comment on the ongoing student protest during a media interaction. His response has triggered a social media debate as several film personalities back the students' demands.

general news.headline: Govt assured paper leak accountability will be discussed: Wangchuk after ending fastSonam Wangchuk, who had been on a hunger strike since June 28, ended his fast after the Centre assured him that Parliament would discuss the NEET-UG paper leak and examination reforms, and that no legal action would be taken against peaceful protesters.

Sonam Wangchuk breaks 26-day fast in presence of JP Nadda, Jitendra SinghClimate activist Sonam Wangchuk had been on an indefinite hunger strike in support of students protesting against irregularities in competitive examinations. He was shifted to Medanta Hospital after spending several days on the fast, with doctors advising close monitoring of his health.

Cabinet to announce tougher paper leak measures today: PM's midnight messagePM Modi said the government's immediate priority after the alleged NEET paper leak was to ensure that students did not lose an academic year.

One district, one demand: CJP's nationwide protest today, 2nd meeting with Centre  Live UpdatesThe Cockroach Party said it aims to 'create a Jantar Mantar' in every district as part of its nationwide protest. A party delegation is also scheduled to meet the Union Ministers today.

When US spy satellites forced India to abort a nuclear testTwo decades after the Pokhran-I nuclear test in 1974, India was ready to test again in 1995. But American spy satellites spotted unusual activity at Pokhran before it could. This is why PM PV Narasimha Rao withheld the final clearance, and the move helped pave the way for the successful Pokhran-II tests three years later.

Jantar Mantar protests may invite legal action, DU warns students, facultyDelhi University has advised students and faculty to stay away from the ongoing protests at Jantar Mantar, warning that participation in unlawful assemblies could invite legal action and adversely affect students' academic progress and future career prospects.

Pellet guns used against protesters in Delhi. Wounds, medical papers reveal truthPellet guns, mostly associated with Jammu and Kashmir, have been used in New Delhi, claim protesters. India Today Digital spoke to a protester whose medico-legal document says he was hit by pellets. He was hit during the CJP's march to Parliament when security personnel allegedly used excessive force.

Education Secretary changed as part of bureaucrats' rejig amid NEET protestsThe Centre has appointed Naresh Pal Gangwar as Secretary of the Department of Higher Education, replacing Vineet Joshi in a bureaucratic reshuffle that comes amid ongoing protests over alleged NEET examination irregularities.

Commonwealth Games Lite: Why 10 sports were axed from Glasgow 2026As the 2026 Commonwealth Games get underway in Glasgow, a drastically reduced sports list tests a new, low-cost formula to save the event—while taking away India's biggest medal-winning events.

IBM earnings disappoint, CEO Arvind Krishna reveals how much software AI can replaceIBM's latest earnings report has made investors concerned about the impact of AI on the company. But IBM CEO Arvind Krishna insists that only a part of its software might be replaced by AI, the rest, he says, could actually help people get AI ready.

US to impose new tariffs of 10% on India over forced labour concernsUS will impose fresh tariffs of 10% and 12.5% on imports from 60 trading partners, including the European Union, from Friday, citing inadequate enforcement of bans on goods produced using forced labour as a temporary 10% global tariff expires.

Adani denies reports of airline plans, says not evaluating entry into aviationAdani Enterprises has denied reports that it is evaluating an entry into the airline business. The clarification follows speculation over rule changes and the group's wider aviation ambitions.

Wanna go on a 'protest date'? Gen Z says see you at Jantar MantarYoung couples are swapping cafe and movie dates for protest dates - showing up for a cause they deeply believe in, alongside someone they care about.

Live: CJP representatives to meet govt delegation at 12.30 pm todayCockroach Janta Party Nationwide Protest Live Updates: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has given a call for a nationwide protest today (July 24), urging people to hold demonstrations in every district. Moreover, a delegation of the outfit is scheduled to meet government representatives at 12.30 pm today at the Constitution Club of India in New Delhi. This came after the CJP's continued insistence that any dialogue with the government should only happen at a "neutral venue." The Abhijeet Dipke-led movement also said Prime Minister Narendra Modi's announcement on action against paper leaks did not address the issue of accountability or its main demand of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over the NEET-related irregularities. The CJP appeared firm in its stance to continue its agitation even as Sonam Wangchuk finally broke his fast after being on a hunger strike for 26 days. The 59-year-old activist announced the end of his fast after Union Ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh met him at the Medanta Hospital in Gurugram around Thursday midnight and conveyed the government's assurances about meeting his demands. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the CJP protest.

I found out why thousands are flooding Jantar Mantar now. It's not Wangchuk or DipkeA protest once confined to a few thousand people has now swelled into a crowd too large to count accurately. Protesters say the July 20 lathi-charge and use of tear gas by Delhi Police and RAF personnel has transformed the frustration of students into anger, triggering a fresh wave of agitation at Jantar Mantar.

Gujarat on red alert: Dangerous vortex to dump extreme rain, next 24 hours criticalA nearly stationary atmospheric vortex is expected to trigger prolonged heavy rain across Gujarat over the next 24 hours, with Saurashtra and Ahmedabad facing a high risk of flooding.

Ranbir-Yash's Ramayana trailer launch postponed, makers plan global rolloutThe makers of Ramayana have postponed the trailer launch after partnering with Sony Pictures Entertainment for a global release. Namit Malhotra said the delay is part of a worldwide strategy and called it a landmark moment for Indian cinema.

Why US-Saudi nuclear pact has Pakistan hopeful, and Israel waryThe US-Saudi nuclear deal approved by American President Donald Trump could allow Riyadh to domestically enrich uranium without several key safeguards Washington has traditionally demanded. The deal has alarmed Israel, and revived questions over whether Saudi Arabia will draw on the nuclear expertise of its long-time strategic partner Pakistan should it decide to go for the bomb.

Sooryavanshi's maiden fifty helps Shreyas Iyer register first win as India captainZIM vs IND, 1st T20I: India's seven-match winless run in T20Is came to an end in Harare on Thursday as Mayank Yadav's hostile spell and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's record-breaking half-century sealed a commanding eight-wicket win over Zimbabwe in the series opener.

Bengaluru CEO rides scooter inside office to celebrate company's 'amazing quarter'A Bengaluru-based CEO rode a scooty through the NoBroker office while celebrating his Packers and Movers team's record quarter, before handing it to the winning employee in a now-viral video.

Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya review: A warm and timely look at India's education systemRelease Date:Fri Jul 24, 2026Adarsh Baal Vidyalaya follows underpaid teachers navigating life inside a crumbling government school. The series uses humour and small heartbreaks to examine India's fractured education system.

17 Delhi Metro stations shut as CJP calls for nationwide protests todayDelhi Metro will shut 17 stations in central Delhi from Friday morning because of security advisories linked to the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar. The move extends a second day of disruption and is expected to hit commuters as demonstrations continue in the capital.

2 BSF jawans shot dead after colleague under custody opens fire at them in BengalA BSF constable under custody allegedly snatched a rifle from a sentry and opened fire on his colleagues at a camp in West Bengal's Malda, killing two personnel and leaving one injured. The accused was possibly suffering from mental stress, preliminary inquiries showed.

Bumper monsoon: 10,000-km-long rain cloud seen from India to South KoreaSatellite imagery has captured a vast monsoon rain band stretching from north India to South Korea. The spell has sharply lifted rainfall across India while prompting flood and landslide warnings in vulnerable regions.

sports.headline: Could the Ashes be played in India? Cricket Australia open to lucrative idea Todd Greenberg said Cricket Australia would consider staging an Australia vs England Test in India. The financial appeal of the Indian market has been on the rise in recent times with the Big Bash League staging a match in Chennai later this year.

Commonwealth Games Lite: Why 10 sports were axed from Glasgow 2026 As the 2026 Commonwealth Games get underway in Glasgow, a drastically reduced sports list tests a new, low-cost formula to save the event—while taking away India's biggest medal-winning events.

From World No.1 to penniless: How tennis icon Arantxa Vicario became bankrupt Arantxa Sanchez Vicario has blamed former husband Josep Santacana for the loss of her fortune and the debt case that followed. The former world No. 1 says she is still repaying the bank, a fall that turned a glittering career into a fight for survival.

India at Commonwealth Games: July 24 schedule, medal events, fixtures in Glasgow India's campaign at the Commonwealth Games 2026 gathers momentum on Friday, July 24, with medal opportunities in para powerlifting and a packed schedule across gymnastics, swimming, lawn bowls and boxing in Glasgow.

Commonwealth Games declared open as Mirabai, Lovlina lead India at Parade of Nations Olympic medallists Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain led India into the Commonwealth Games 2026 opening ceremony to a thunderous reception as King Charles III officially declared the Games open at the OVO Hydro in Glasgow on Wednesday.

Childhood dream kept me motivated: Mayank Yadav 2.0 impresses on injury comeback India pacer Mayank Yadav has opened up on the mental toll of his two-year injury layoff, describing the prolonged absence from international cricket as the toughest phase of his young career after making an impressive return against Zimbabwe in the first T20I on Thursday.

Highlights: Lovlina, Mirabai lead India's contingent in style Commonwealth Games 2026 Highlights: The Commonwealth Games 2026 began with a star-studded opening ceremony at Glasgow's OVO Hydro Arena. Around 3,000 athletes from 74 nations, including a strong Indian contingent led by Mirabai Chanu, will mark the start of the 11-day spectacle.

Our generation deserves every opportunity to learn: Shubman Gill on NEET protests Shubman Gill shared a message calling for compassion, mutual respect and greater opportunities for young people as student protests continue, while stressing the importance of education and peaceful expression.

PV Sindhu calls out China Open scheduling after Chen Yufei ends her campaign PV Sindhu questioned the China Open scheduling after her second-round exit on Thursday, highlighting the disparity in recovery time between players with a tongue-in-cheek post on social media following her defeat to home favourite Chen Yufei.

Hard work must be rewarded, cheating has no place: Sachin amid NEET protests Batting great Sachin Tendulkar on Thursday called for a transparent and merit-based system amid the ongoing student protests, saying young people should never feel that their hard work has gone unrewarded.

Could not be happier: Shreyas Iyer savours his first win as India's T20I captain IND vs ZIM: Shreyas Iyer celebrated his maiden win as India's T20I captain after a dominant victory over Zimbabwe, heaping praise on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, whose record-breaking 18-ball fifty powered India to a comfortable chase.

India's Putul Sonowal stuns 2023 world champion Ryan Bester on CWG debutIndia's lawn bowls campaign at the Glasgow Commonwealth Games got off to a flying start on Thursday as Putul Sonowal produced one of the biggest upsets of the opening day, defeating 2023 world champion Ryan Bester of Canada in a tie-break in the Men's Singles.

Sooryavanshi's maiden fifty helps Shreyas Iyer register first win as India captainZIM vs IND, 1st T20I: India's seven-match winless run in T20Is came to an end in Harare on Thursday as Mayank Yadav's hostile spell and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's record-breaking half-century sealed a commanding eight-wicket win over Zimbabwe in the series opener.

Sooryavanshi pips Sachin to become youngest to score an international fiftyVaibhav Sooryavanshi scripted history by becoming the youngest player from a Full Member nation to score an international fifty. The 15-year-old surpassed Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record with a sparkling knock against Zimbabwe in the T20I in Harare.

India beat Malta in Bowls women's pairs event in Glasgow CWG campaign openerIndia began their Glasgow Commonwealth Games campaign with a tense tie-break win over Malta in the women's pairs lawn bowls event on Thursday. Rupa Rani Tirkey (lead) and Pinki (skip) won the opening set 7-1 before Malta took the second 4-3 to force a tie-break.

WTC Final: The Oval returns as host for 2027 title clashThe ICC has confirmed The Oval as the venue for the 2027 World Test Championship final in London. The decision comes as the qualification race remains open, with all nine Test nations still in contention.

IND vs ZIM: Ashok Sharma earns maiden India cap, Mayank Yadav returns after 21 monthsIND vs ZIM, 1st T20I Playing XIs: Ashok Sharma's impressive rise from IPL sensation to India international continued as the Rajasthan pacer made his T20I debut against Zimbabwe. India also welcomed back express quick Mayank Yadav after a 21-month injury-enforced absence.

Ashes 2027 schedule announced, Trent Bridge returns to host series openerThe ECB has scheduled the men's and women's Ashes to run in parallel again in 2027. Trent Bridge and Headingley will serve as the openers for the men's and women's Ashes respectively.

Commonwealth Games 2026 Opening Ceremony: Timing, TV Channel, Live StreamingThe Commonwealth Games 2026 open in Glasgow with a star-studded ceremony at the OVO Hydro. The event launches 11 days of competition and puts India in focus with Mirabai Chanu leading the contingent.

CJP Protest: Students use Norway's viral Viking Row with a twist in MumbaiThe students in Mumbai, who are part of the CJP protests, decided to use the Norway football team's iconic Viking Row as a medium to voice their concerns in Mumbai.

How Lovlina Borgohain got India CWG 2026 medal before opening ceremonyLovlina Borgohain moved straight into the Commonwealth Games 2026 women's 75kg semifinals after a bye. That put India on the medals table before the opening ceremony, with at least bronze already assured.

Indian judoka Arun Kumar cut from CWG 2026 after doping test failureIndia judoka Arun Kumar has been ruled out of the Commonwealth Games after an adverse analytical finding in an out-of-competition test. The late notice leaves India without a replacement in the men's 73kg event and strips a medal hope on the eve of competition.

Keep faith in government: Dhawan urges patience from students amid protestsShikhar Dhawan asked protesting students to stay patient and trust the government amid the CJP protests in Delhi. The statement comes after the demonstrations over the alleged NEET-UG 2026 paper leak and examination irregularities have continued to garner nationwide attention.

Argentina fans launch petition to replay the World Cup 2026 final. Here's whyArgentina supporters have launched a petition asking FIFA to replay the 2026 World Cup final after the loss to Spain. The campaign attacks the officiating of referee Slavko Vincic, calling it biased.

Olympic gold medallist Abhinav Bindra backs students amid protestsOlympic gold medalist Abhinav Bindra shared a message on the ongoing student protests at Delhi's Jantar Mantar, urging India to strengthen its education system. His remarks come as nationwide demonstrations over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak continue to intensify.

Ramos takes a jibe at Messi by recreating Argentina star's iconic World Cup picAfter Spain beat Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final, Sergio Ramos recreated Lionel Messi's viral trophy-sleeping photo to take a light-hearted jibe at the Argentina captain. Messi and Co. were beaten by Spin 1-0 in the showdown in New York.

Rodri set for surgery after Spain's World Cup win as Real Madrid links persistSpain's World Cup-winning captain Rodri is set to undergo back surgery after a stellar tournament that earned him the Golden Ball. The latest injury setback comes as uncertainty continues over his Manchester City future, with contract talks unresolved and Real Madrid links refusing to fade.

Messi's Inter Miami faces MLS investigation over alleged Casemiro deal tamperingInter Miami's signing of Casemiro has quickly come under scrutiny after MLS launched a tampering investigation into the transfer. The probe centres on discovery rights, reviving questions over the club's transfer dealings despite securing another marquee signing alongside Lionel Messi.

Don't expect medal rush: Why India could be in for a quiet CWG 2026India has been amongst the medal when it comes to the Commonwealth Games in recent times, but this time the excitement has been watered down. A reduced roster of events and with some historic medal-winning events missing, India could be in for a quiet outing in Glasgow this year.

CWG 2026: India's full schedule, list of athletes, complete event listIndia's Commonwealth Games 2026 campaign begins on July 23 with a 126-member contingent aiming to return with around 30 medals in Glasgow. Here's the complete date-wise schedule featuring Neeraj Chopra, Mirabai Chanu, Lovlina Borgohain, Murali Sreeshankar, Tejaswin Shankar and every major Indian medal contender.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
